meaning: n.舐食流体食物的人或动物
examples: Slivers are fed through the lapper that smooths them to a thin layer of fibers which is wound onto a roll.
Aiming at the poor web uniformity of non-woven fabrics produced by traditional cross lapper, the motion control bus SERCOS is applied in the digital servo system of the W1251 cross lapper.
Lapper was born almost without arms and very short legs - disabilities similar to those caused by Thalidomide.
Quinn recently made a 12 ft version of one of his marble sculptures "Alison Lapper Pregnant", a limbless woman who is eight months pregnant.
Lapper is particularly influenced by the Venus de Milo, which is widely regarded as a classical depiction of female beauty despite having no arms.
